ai_insights.executive_summary: CHC delivered mixed results in February 2026, with three clinics posting gains in scheduler compliance and patient flow, but two locations logging concerning overtime patterns. The network's composite performance score averaged 37.6 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), reflecting operational friction points that warrant targeted intervention alongside celebrating genuine momentum in areas like Blue Pod's delay reduction and Muskegon's compliance climb.
highlights: Blue Pod cut average patient delay from 43.6 minutes in January to 38.9 minutes in February—a 10.8% improvement that now sits only 72% above the company average of 22.6 minutes, signaling real progress in throughput. Muskegon simultaneously lifted scheduler compliance from 61.1% in January to 70.6% in February, a 15.6% gain that nearly matched the company average of 70.92%, while maintaining the lowest average delay in the network at 10.6 minutes (company average: 22.55 minutes).
concerns: We noticed Blue Pod's average delay, while improved, remains the widest gap in the network at 38.9 minutes against the company average of 22.55 minutes—a 73% differential that warrants attention to scheduling velocity and chair-to-patient assignment flow. At the same time, two clinics (Red Pod and Muskegon) are logging 3+ treatments past closing daily, and LHCP's chair utilization sits at 70.2% alongside a four-month declining pattern in minutes past close per patient (3-month average: 39.2 minutes); these patterns suggest potential understaffing or scheduling misalignment rather than capacity constraints.
recommendations: We recommend Blue Pod conduct a structured audit of its iOptimize scheduling template compliance and nurse-to-chair assignment workflow in the next 30 days, with specific focus on slot-fill sequencing and real-time delay flagging during peak hours. Given that Blue Pod's scheduler compliance stands at 73.5%—3.4 percentage points above company average—but delay sits 73% above benchmark, the gap is not adherence to recommendations but rather the recommendations themselves or their execution; a joint session with iOptimize support to pilot workflow adjustments will clarify whether template updates or on-site coaching is the right lever.

ai_insights.executive_summary: CHC delivered mixed results in March 2026, with Muskegon showing strong operational momentum while LHCP and its sub-clinics remain below the network average performance score of 50. Muskegon's treatments past closing fell 54.8% from 3.3 in February to 1.5 in March, and chair utilization rose 8.4% from 75.8% to 82.1%, yet nurse utilization declined across a consistent five-month downward trend. LHCP's scheduler compliance improved 6.2% from 71.3% in February to 75.7% in March, but the network still faces a critical patient delay challenge, particularly at LHCP-Blue.
highlights: Muskegon achieved a 54.8% reduction in daily treatments running past closing, dropping from 3.3 in February to 1.5 in March, demonstrating improved scheduling discipline and patient flow management. Chair utilization at Muskegon climbed 8.4% from 75.8% in February to 82.1% in March, now exceeding the company average of 75.77% and reflecting more effective asset deployment. LHCP's scheduler compliance rose 6.2% from 71.3% in February to 75.7% in March, aligning closely with the company average of 74.75% and marking the third consecutive month of improvement at that clinic.
concerns: We noticed that Muskegon's minutes past close per patient rose 27.8% from 32.7 in February to 41.8 in March, even as the number of treatments running late declined, suggesting treatments that do extend past closing are taking longer to complete. This metric, alongside a 5-month declining trend in nurse utilization (3-month average 73.1%), warrants exploration of whether staffing levels are optimally matched to the current treatment volume. LHCP-Blue presents the most pressing opportunity: its average patient delay stands at 43.2 minutes against the company average of 22.78 minutes, a gap of approximately 90%, and we would benefit from a collaborative review of how iAssign assignments align with actual chair and nurse capacity at that location.
recommendations: We recommend prioritizing a scheduling template audit at LHCP-Blue to investigate why average delays are nearly double the company benchmark of 22.78 minutes. Specifically, we suggest reviewing how iAssign recommendations are being followed in practice and whether the current nurse-to-chair ratio (not provided for the sub-clinic) may be creating bottlenecks; this targeted review will help us unlock meaningful improvements in patient flow without requiring significant resource investment.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
